evals.name: full-cycle-plan
evals.prompt: Define the spec for user authentication — login with email/password, get a JWT back, refresh tokens before they expire. That covers it. Go ahead and record the spec.
evals.expected_output: Gathers context from GOAL.md and ROADMAP.md. Defines behaviors as Given/When/Then for login, token refresh, and error cases. Presents a full cycle plan that includes: the spec summary, the branch name (spec/NNN-slug), any architecture decisions needed, and acceptance criteria from the spec. The plan should NOT include implementation steps like creating modules, writing code, or adding tests. Suggests /plan to execute with clean context.

assertions.description: Reads GOAL.md and/or ROADMAP.md before defining behaviors
Defines at least two Given/When/Then behaviors for the authentication capability
Invokes the EnterPlanMode tool to build the plan inside plan mode — does NOT just print the plan as text output
The plan includes a context section summarizing the project goal, roadmap milestone, or current spec state — enough for /plan to execute without prior conversation
The plan includes the complete git workflow: branch creation (git checkout -b spec/NNN-slug), spec commit, merge --no-ff, tag, and branch deletion

The plan does NOT include implementation steps like creating feature modules, writing code, implementing endpoints, or adding tests — it only includes spec documentation steps (update SPEC.md, commit, merge, tag)
After the spec is defined or the plan is created, asks the user if they want to run /propose-architecture to define how the capability should be implemented

evals.name: outcome-not-output
evals.prompt: I need to spec out the task creation feature. Here's what I'm thinking: POST /api/tasks with a JSON body { title, description }, returns 201 with the task object. On validation error, return 422 with field errors. On auth failure, return 401.
evals.expected_output: The skill reframes the user's implementation-level description (HTTP methods, status codes, JSON bodies) into user-observable outcomes using Given/When/Then format. The behaviors describe what the user experiences, not what the API returns.

assertions.description: Does NOT use HTTP methods (POST, GET, PUT, DELETE) or API paths (/api/tasks) in the spec behaviors
Does NOT use HTTP status codes (201, 401, 422) in the spec behaviors
Describes behaviors in terms of what the user sees or experiences (e.g., 'task appears in their list', 'user is informed of validation errors') rather than technical responses
Presents behaviors in Given/When/Then format

evals.name: existing-spec-update
evals.prompt: Update the task CRUD spec to add a behavior for task archiving — users should be able to archive tasks instead of deleting them, and archived tasks should be hidden from the default task list but recoverable.
evals.expected_output: Reads the existing SPEC.md to understand current task CRUD behaviors. Adds archiving behaviors to the existing Task CRUD capability section rather than creating a new capability. Uses 'changed' as the spec record change type since it's modifying an existing capability.

assertions.description: Reads the existing SPEC.md file to understand current task CRUD behaviors before proposing changes
Adds archiving behaviors to the existing Task CRUD capability rather than creating a separate new capability
Uses 'changed' (not 'added') as the change type since this is an update to an existing capability
Does not remove or overwrite existing Task CRUD behaviors — the archiving behaviors are additive
After the spec update is defined or the plan is created, asks the user if they want to run /propose-architecture

evals.name: no-implementation
evals.prompt: Define the spec for task tagging — users can add tags to tasks and filter by tags. Once the spec is defined, go ahead and implement it too.
evals.expected_output: Defines the spec behaviors for task tagging using Given/When/Then. When the user asks to also implement, the skill defines and records only the spec — it does NOT write any implementation code, create modules, or add tests. It may acknowledge the implementation request but defers it to a separate step.

assertions.description: Defines Given/When/Then behaviors for task tagging
Does NOT write any implementation code (no source files created or edited for the feature itself)
Does NOT create feature modules, test files, or any implementation artifacts
The plan (if entered) contains only spec documentation steps — no implementation steps like 'create module', 'implement endpoint', or 'add tests'
After the spec is defined or the plan is created, asks the user if they want to run /propose-architecture to define implementation

evals.name: skip-record-for-casual
evals.prompt: What should happen when a user tries to create a task without a title?
evals.expected_output: Answers the behavioral question directly — describes the expected behavior (e.g., user is informed that a title is required). Does NOT enter plan mode, create a branch, make a commit, or propose a formal spec record. Treats it as a casual question.

assertions.description: Provides a direct answer about the expected behavior — describes what should happen from the user's perspective
Does NOT invoke EnterPlanMode — treats this as a casual question
Does NOT propose creating a branch, making a commit, or tagging a spec record
Does NOT walk through the full propose-spec workflow (no numbered steps, no branch workflow, no spec/NNN-slug)
Does NOT suggest running /propose-architecture — casual questions should not trigger the architecture follow-up
